citation.anchor: Addition and subtraction (add, sub) are supported

preconditions: Every selected A-unit arithmetic encoding and source register is legal
inputs: Base and boundary vectors for add/subtract, +1/-1, signed immediates, shifted-add, and addp4 forms
expected_results: All arithmetic results equal independent modulo or pointer-address goldens
permitted_state_changes: Selected destination GR and NaT bit
required_unchanged_state: Sources, unrelated registers, predicates, and memory
cases.committed_prefix: Each enabled arithmetic form commits one destination update

oracle.derivation: Python unbounded arithmetic, explicit 64-bit reduction, and literal pointer-region construction define results without production helpers

preconditions: Logical register/immediate and movl forms occupy legal slots
inputs: Overlapping/complementary patterns, signed immediate extremes, and two full-width movl constants
expected_results: Register, complement, immediate, and constant-construction results match literal bit models
cases.committed_prefix: One complete logical or constant result commits

oracle.derivation: Test-owned Python bit operations and literal immediate reconstruction provide the oracle

preconditions: mpy4 and mpyshl4 source halves are explicit
expected_results: Full 32x32 and shifted partial products match exact Python products

oracle.derivation: Explicit 32-bit slicing and Python multiplication define both products

preconditions: Source FR significands contain canonical integer operands
inputs: Signed/unsigned multiplicands and a zero-extended addend
expected_results: Low and signed/unsigned high halves match the exact 128-bit multiply-add
required_unchanged_state: Source FRs and unrelated FPSR state
fault_priority: False qp suppresses disabled checks; enabled XMA reports the first disabled FR operand
cases.restart_action: Faulting inputs restart without an FR write

oracle.derivation: Literal operands and exact 128-bit integer products/addends define each selected half independently

citation.anchor: Four classes of instructions are defined

preconditions: Field positions/lengths and variable counts are legal
inputs: Interior and boundary logical/arithmetic shifts, funnel shifts, extracts, deposits, and every implemented extension width
expected_results: All shifted, masked, merged, and extended values equal independent bit models

oracle.derivation: Test-owned masks, concatenation, sign extension, and count-boundary rules define every result

preconditions: Bit patterns and byte/halfword lanes are distinguishable
inputs: Zero/all-one/alternating popcnt/clz inputs plus mux and left/right czx patterns
expected_results: Counts, indices, and selected lanes match independent literal goldens
allowed_results: Exact count, index, or arranged 64-bit value

oracle.derivation: Python bit counts and test-owned lane permutations/scans define exact results

preconditions: Every scalar form has explicit predicate, NaT, alias, and legal-slot fixtures
inputs: All 40 false qualifiers, all 40 NaT rows, 18 destination/source aliases, every A form in M and I slots, and enabled/nullified reserved POPCNT
expected_results: Exact non-effects, NaT propagation/clearing, alias results, slot equivalence, and precise Illegal Operation state
allowed_results: Exact legal result/NaT, unchanged poisoned target, or declared fault vector/state
fault_priority: False qp suppresses the reserved field; enabled reserved POPCNT faults before target/suffix effects
cases.committed_prefix: Legal writes commit once and the reserved fault preserves its prefix
cases.restart_action: The reserved encoding reports exact IIP and slot RI

oracle.derivation: Literal encodings, values, NaT placement, vector addresses, and slot expectations are owned by the test

preconditions: The declarative test inventory exactly equals the translator's 40 scalar case labels
inputs: Two deterministic generated base/boundary vectors for every admitted opcode
expected_results: All 80 guest results and all 40 source-count-based NaT outcomes equal independent models
cases.committed_prefix: Each generated row commits one result

oracle.derivation: Declarative encoders and Python arithmetic/bit models are separate from production decoder and TCG tables
